title: argyrolobium rupestre subsp. kilimandscharicum
intro: Argyrolobium rupestre subsp. kilimandscharicum, also known as Kilimanjaro argyrolobium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is native to East Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Argyrolobium rupestre subsp. kilimandscharicum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used to make various medicinal preparations.
